insight: For Senegal, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, the team has seen a slight decline in momentum after a hydration break, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, indicating that they tend to lose a bit of steam after stopping play. However, the numbers also suggest that when Senegal is pressing before a break, they tend to lose even more momentum afterwards, with an average change of -0.100 per minute, and they have not been able to regain their footing after such breaks. In contrast, when they are not pressing before a break, they are able to maintain their momentum, with no average change, which could inform their strategy going forward.
